# Pay-per-Pick Inquiry — Lager24 Logistics AG

From: Anna Becker, CFO, Lager24 Logistics AG, Frankfurt
To: UNIFI Deal Desk
Subject: Inquiry for Pay-per-Pick offer — heavy-parcel automation lane

Dear UNIFI team,

Lager24 Logistics is a mid-market parcel-handling operation. We are launching an automated lane for heavier parcels in our Frankfurt hub and would like to evaluate a Pay-per-Pick arrangement instead of taking on additional leasing debt.

We plan to deploy 8 robots, handling around 1.5 million picks per month at base volume. Our package mix is heavier than typical for e-commerce: 20 percent light items below 1 kg, 50 percent medium items between 1 and 3 kg, and 30 percent heavy items above 3 kg.

We have a pronounced Black Friday peak each year: roughly 3 times our base volume for about 6 weeks. Outside that window, volume is steady.

We are open on robot type — please advise which one fits our load profile best. We would prefer a 3-year contract term. Flexibility is high-priority: this lane is new for us, and if the business case shifts we may want to redirect capital to other parts of our network without paying a heavy exit penalty.

Please provide a structured offer with price per pick, expected monthly cost at base and at peak, contract clauses suited to our load profile and our flexibility needs, and a clear cash-flow comparison against a classical leasing alternative.

Best regards,
Anna Becker
CFO, Lager24 Logistics AG
